notes.en: Warm, dry conditions; sheltered plant canopies. Populations explode in the dry season and in greenhouse / polytunnel cultivation. Indiscriminate broad-spectrum insecticide use promotes resistance.

description.en: Tiny (1 mm) sap-sucking insects on the undersides of leaves that release a white waxy dust on disturbance. Direct feeding causes leaf yellowing, honeydew deposits that support sooty mould, and growth reduction. **More critically, Bemisia tabaci is the vector of several begomoviruses** — tomato yellow leaf curl virus (TYLCV), Sri Lankan cassava mosaic virus (SLCMV), leaf curl viruses of chilli and okra, and cucurbit yellows complexes — making whitefly management the primary lever for controlling these diseases. Management: reflective mulches, yellow sticky traps (4–5 per 100 m² of nursery), insect-proof nursery covers, biological control (*Encarsia formosa*, *Eretmocerus* parasitoids), selective insecticides (rotate MoA groups to delay resistance), and quick removal of alternate-host weeds.

description.quote: the silverleaf whitefly, Bemisia tabaci biotype B. Whiteflies can inoculate a tomato plant with TYLCV after 15 min of feeding

favored_conditions.quote: Only Asia II 1 whiteflies were able to transmit this virus efficiently among three widely distributed whitefly species in Asia
